labels.allDataDesc: Replaces entire catalog with selected data
labels.orbitalOnlyDesc: Updates TLEs while preserving satellite metadata

errorMsgs.Forbidden: CelesTrak rejected the request (HTTP 403). This is usually a Cloudflare rate limit; wait a minute and try again.
errorMsgs.RateLimited: Too many catalog requests. Please wait a minute before trying again.
errorMsgs.ServerError: CelesTrak is temporarily unavailable. Please try again in a few minutes.
errorMsgs.NetworkError: Could not reach CelesTrak. Check your network connection.

help.overview: The Catalog Browser swaps the satellite catalog you are viewing. Pick from curated lists (a constellation, a debris cloud, a mission category) or full catalogs from CelesTrak and JSC Vimpel, and the scene reloads with exactly those objects.
help.imgAlt: The Catalog Browser menu with its load mode toggle and catalog list
help.imgCaption: Pick a load mode, then click any catalog to load it.
help.modes: All Data replaces the entire catalog with the selected data set, including names, types, and metadata. Orbital Only keeps every satellite you already have and only refreshes their orbital elements, which is the safer choice when you have edited objects or rely on existing metadata.
help.howToUse:
1. Open the Catalog Browser from the bottom menu (Catalog mode).
  2. Choose All Data or Orbital Only with the toggle; the description below it explains the current mode.
  3. Click a catalog in the categorized list, such as Space Stations, Starlink, or COSMOS 1408 Debris. A toast confirms when it loads.
  4. Load Default Catalog to return to the standard full catalog.
help.tip1: Every catalog is also available from the command palette as a Load Catalog command, so you can switch without opening the menu.
help.tip2: Loading a small catalog dramatically improves performance on slower machines; the 100 Brightest list is a good minimal set.
help.tip3: Catalog downloads come from CelesTrak and can be rate limited; if a load fails with an HTTP 403 message, wait a minute and try again.
